steps.title: Identify the structure around the blank
steps.content:
Left side: the noun “Police” (part of the noun phrase “the International Criminal Police”).
Right side: the prepositional phrase “in 1923”.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A pair of em dashes sets off a nonessential appositive.
Choice A provides the closing em dash needed to match the opening em dash after Interpol.
Choices B, C, and D either mismatch the opening dash, leave the appositive unclosed, or introduce a colon that does not fit the sentence structure.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
